title: Aracium alpinum
intro: Aracium alpinum, commonly known as the Wollemi Pine, is a rare and ancient gymnosperm plant native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory conditions, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Aracium alpinum include compounds such as podophyllotoxin, lignans, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the Wollemi Pine has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into modern supplements and pharmaceuticals.
taxonomy: Aracium alpinum is classified as a plant species within the family Araceae. This family, commonly known as the arum family, comprises over 2,600 species of plants. The classification of Aracium alpinum is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta. Aracium alpinum is a unique plant species that belongs to this specific classification.
